title: corydalis umbrosa
intro: Corydalis umbrosa, also known as the least corydalis or woodland corydalis, is a flowering plant species in the family Papaveraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties, which are attributed to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids such as corynoline and protopine. Consuming corydalis umbrosa has been associated with various health benefits, including relief from pain and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and reduced inflammation. Corydalis umbrosa is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal effects.
